summary: Typosquat of @livekit/agents with install-time beacon to lookalike domain
details: Package `livekit-agents` impersonates the legitimate scoped LiveKit SDK `@livekit/agents`. The README copies the real LiveKit Agents description and links to docs.livekit.io/agents, but the shipped module is a one-line stub (`greet(name){ return "Hello, " + name; }`) with no LiveKit functionality. A `postinstall` script (`postinstall.js`) issues an HTTPS POST to `livekit-agents.xyz/api/metrics` carrying package version, ISO timestamp, Node version, platform and arch; the same beacon (type `cli_run`) also fires from the package's `bin` entry. The destination `livekit-agents.xyz` is an attacker-registered lookalike of LiveKit's real domain `livekit.io`. The combination of brand-impersonating name, copied README, non-functional payload, and install-time beacon to an attacker-controlled lookalike confirms an active typosquat: the install fingerprints mis-installations (platform/arch/Node version) to the attacker, enabling targeted follow-on versions.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
